diagrams.title: RSA Key Generation and Encryption Flow
diagrams.description: Flow diagram showing RSA key generation (prime selection → n = pq → φ(n) → e,d calculation), then encryption/decryption process
elements: Compute private exponent d = e⁻¹ mod φ(n)
diagrams.purpose: Shows the complete RSA process and highlights where security breaks if n is factored

diagrams.title: RSA Security Attack Vectors
diagrams.description: Visual categorization of different attack types on RSA with their attack surfaces and countermeasures
elements: Mathematical Attacks: Integer factorization (GNFS), Wiener's attack (small d), Broadcast attack (small e)
Side-Channel Attacks: Timing attacks, power analysis, fault injection
Padding Attacks: Bleichenbacher's CCA, oracle attacks
Implementation Attacks: Weak RNG, key reuse, partial key exposure
diagrams.purpose: Maps all attack vectors to help students understand defense-in-depth approach

diagrams.title: Key Size Security Timeline
diagrams.description: Timeline showing when different RSA key sizes became/will become insecure, based on GNFS progress
diagrams.purpose: Visual guide to key size recommendations and historical factorization progress
